title: Physical Analyzer / Reader review pass
action: Open the UFDR in Physical Analyzer (or Reader for review-only). Walk the standard data categories — Calls, Messages, Chats, Contacts, Locations, Web History, Installed Applications, Device Locations, Wireless Networks. Tag entries that corroborate the case theory. Build a timeline view from Locations + Calls + Chats and export it for the report bundle.
rationale: Mobile cases are won on the cross-app timeline (a chat at 14:02, a tower hit at 14:05, a navigation request at 14:07). Physical Analyzer's Timeline view stitches per-app records into one chronology and is the single most defensible analysis artefact in a mobile exam.
expected_result: Physical Analyzer / Reader case opens cleanly; categories populate; timeline view renders; tagged items export to the report bundle as a UFED Reader package.

cellebrite.action: Physical Analyzer → Open Case → point at the UFDR. Use the Conversation View on Chats / Messages for context-preserved threads. Switch to Timeline View (Analyzed Data → Timeline) for the cross-app chronology; right-click → Add Tag on relevant entries. Report → Generate Report → UFED Reader to ship a self-contained review bundle to opposing counsel or the requestor.
cellebrite.ui_path: Physical Analyzer > Open Case > [UFDR] > Analyzed Data > Timeline
cellebrite.notes: Reader (the free viewer) covers most review needs; reserve Physical Analyzer for cases needing decoding, decryption, or chain-of-custody-logged tagging. Reader bundles include hash verification of the source UFDR — useful for opposing-counsel deliveries.
